businessSummary.oneLiner: The undisputed king of AI computing — dominating global AI infrastructure with data center GPUs and AI accelerators
businessSummary.description: NVIDIA is the world's largest semiconductor design company, with AI training and inference data center accelerators as its core revenue driver. Q4 FY2026 revenue $68.13B (+73% YoY), EPS $1.62. Q1 FY2027 guidance of $78B (±2%) beat consensus by $5.4B. FY2026 annual revenue $215.9B (+65.5%). Mar 2 close $182.48 (+2.99%) recovering some post-earnings decline. Announced $2B investments each in Lumentum and Coherent for photonics AI infrastructure. GTC 2026 (Mar 16-19) Rubin GPU reveal imminent. P/E 37.24x, Forward P/E 22.14x, PEG 0.54.
howTheyMakeMoney: Data Center: AI training/inference GPUs (H100, H200, Blackwell B200/GB200/GB300) — ~91% of revenue
Gaming: GeForce RTX series GPUs and DLSS 4 technology
Automotive: DRIVE platform autonomous driving and vehicle AI chips
Software/Platform: CUDA ecosystem, NVIDIA AI Enterprise, Omniverse
keyProducts: Blackwell Ultra GB300: Latest AI accelerator, 50x throughput vs Hopper, shipments projected to surge 129% YoY in 2026
Blackwell B200/GB200: Sold-out demand across hyperscalers
H100/H200 Tensor Core GPU: AI industry standard
Rubin R200: Next-gen GPU, official reveal at GTC 2026 (Mar 16-19)
DGX/HGX Systems: Turnkey servers for large-scale AI training

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Slightly up on +2.99% rebound. Still reasonable relative to growth

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2027 EPS $7.87. PEG 0.54 signals undervaluation vs growth

keyMetrics.name: Operating Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Industry-best. Reflects dominant position in AI accelerators

keyMetrics.name: ROE
keyMetrics.interpretation: Ultra-high profitability with maximum capital efficiency

keyMetrics.name: PEG
keyMetrics.interpretation: Clearly undervalued relative to growth. Core of Goldman, JPMorgan bull thesis

growthDrivers.title: Lumentum & Coherent $2B Each — Photonics Strategy for AI Processors
growthDrivers.description: NVIDIA investing $2B each in photonic product makers Lumentum and Coherent, integrating photonic technology into data center chips for next-gen AI infrastructure.

growthDrivers.title: GTC 2026 (Mar 16-19) — Rubin R200 Reveal D-14
growthDrivers.description: Jensen Huang to officially unveil next-gen Rubin (R200) architecture at GTC San Jose. 5x performance over Blackwell. Event-driven re-rating realistic.

growthDrivers.title: Q4 FY2026 Record Earnings — 13 Consecutive Beats, Q1 Guide $78B
growthDrivers.description: Revenue $68.13B (+73% YoY), EPS $1.62. Q1 FY2027 guidance $78B (±2%) beat consensus by $5.4B. 'Customers are racing to invest in AI' — Jensen Huang.

growthDrivers.title: Hyperscaler CapEx ~$700B — AI Infrastructure Spending Accelerating
growthDrivers.description: Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, Microsoft combined 2026 CapEx ~$700B. Multi-year, millions-of-GPUs partnership with Meta.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: CUDA ecosystem (15M+ developers), annual architecture refresh, overwhelming NVLink performance, 80%+ AI accelerator market share maintaining de facto monopoly.

moats.description: CUDA has 15+ year history. 15M+ developers optimized for NVIDIA.
Hopper→Blackwell→Rubin annual architecture refresh. GB300 is 50x more efficient than Hopper.
Secured majority of TSMC's advanced CoWoS packaging capacity.
Multi-year partnerships with Meta, OpenAI, Eli Lilly and others.

competitors.name: AMD
competitors.detail: MI300X/MI350 at 10-12% AI accelerator market share. MI450 (2nm) planned.

competitors.name: Intel
competitors.detail: Gaudi 3 launched but negligible market share.

competitors.name: Custom Silicon
competitors.detail: Google TPU, Amazon Trainium, Microsoft Maia — hyperscalers accelerating in-house chip development.

competitors.name: Huawei Ascend
competitors.detail: NVIDIA alternative in China. Export restrictions limiting NVIDIA's China share.

recentNews.headline: NVIDIA Invests $2B Each in Lumentum and Coherent — Photonics AI Strategy
recentNews.significance: Integrating optical technology into data center chips for next-gen AI infrastructure competitiveness.

recentNews.headline: CNBC: 'NVDA is a Coiled Spring' After Blockbuster Quarter
recentNews.significance: Q1 guidance $78B beat consensus by $5.4B yet stock flat. GTC expected to be the rebound trigger.

recentNews.headline: Trump Tariffs and Export Controls — NVDA Down -7.2% Over 3 Days
recentNews.significance: Semiconductor tariffs (15-20%) and tightened AI chip export restrictions weighing on shares.

recentNews.headline: NVDA +2.99% Rebound — $182.48 Recovering Post-Earnings Decline
recentNews.significance: First rebound after 3-day -7.2% drop. Lumentum/Coherent investment announcement improved sentiment.

recentNews.headline: GTC 2026 (Mar 16-19) Confirmed — Rubin GPU Reveal, GB300 Shipment Surge
recentNews.significance: Rubin R200 and Feynman architecture reveal at GTC San Jose. GB300 shipments up 129% YoY in 2026.

analystOpinions.notableComment: 36 of 38 analysts Buy. Citi $300, Bernstein $300, JPMorgan $265 ('coiled spring'), Morgan Stanley $260. Forward P/E 22x with PEG 0.54 — consensus is 'historically cheap'.

risks.title: Trump Tariffs and Tightening Chip Export Controls
risks.description: Proposed 15-20% semiconductor tariffs and stricter AI chip restrictions to China directly threaten supply chain costs and constrain China revenue.

risks.title: CoreWeave Miss — Customer Concentration Risk
risks.description: CoreWeave's guidance shortfall revived questions about NVDA's dependence on a narrow set of cloud customers.

risks.title: Below 50-Day SMA — Technical Weakness Persists
risks.description: Shares below 50-day SMA. Short-term moving average signals skew bearish, though 200-day MA recovered.

risks.title: Inflation Surprise — Rate Cut Expectations Pushed Out
risks.description: PPI above consensus. Higher-for-longer rates compress growth stock multiples.

risks.title: Big Tech Custom Chips Accelerating
risks.description: Google TPU v6, Amazon Trainium3, Microsoft Maia scaling. Could erode NVIDIA's addressable market among hyperscalers.

buyReasons.title: GTC 2026 (Mar 16-19) — Major Catalyst Two Weeks Out
buyReasons.rationale: Jensen Huang to officially unveil Rubin R200 with 5x performance over Blackwell. Event-driven re-rating realistic.

buyReasons.title: Forward P/E 22x, PEG 0.54 — Historically Cheap
buyReasons.rationale: Post-earnings pullback pushed Forward P/E to 22x. PEG 0.54 for a 55%+ growth company is rare by any historical measure.

buyReasons.title: Lumentum & Coherent $4B Strategic Investment — Long-Term Vision
buyReasons.rationale: Photonics investment strengthens next-gen data center chip competitiveness. Reinforces long-term technology leadership commitment.

buyReasons.title: 36 of 38 Analysts Buy — Consensus $263 (+44%)
buyReasons.rationale: Overwhelming buy-side support. GB300 shipments up 129% YoY, agentic AI demand accelerating.

overallOpinion: Mar 2 close $182.48 (+2.99%). First rebound after 3-day -7.2% decline. $2B each investment in Lumentum and Coherent expands photonics AI infrastructure strategy. GTC 2026 (3/16) Rubin R200 reveal D-14.
36 of 38 analysts Buy. Citi and Bernstein $300, JPMorgan $265. Forward P/E 22x, PEG 0.54 — historically cheap for a 55% grower. Q1 guidance $78B confirms earnings momentum.
$182.48, recovered above 200-day MA, testing 50-day $186. RSI neutral. Technical rebound momentum forming ahead of GTC.
